ip_outcome_note: Waltrill is a confirmed named character from Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles: Echoes of Time (2009, Nintendo DS/Wii). He is a Yuke mage who serves as a mentor and parental figure to the young Yuke Norschtalen, whom he found lost in the forest and raised in the village. The card name directly matches the character name; the depicted chibi dark-robed mage with pointed hat, book, and lantern is consistent with his mage-scholar archetype. Internal Square Enix IP — no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Opus VIII (released March 2019) is a multi-title FFTCG set drawing characters from across the Final Fantasy franchise including FFVII, FFIX, FFXI, FFXIV: Stormblood, and FFXV, among others — rather than a single-game themed set [Square Enix FFTCG Opus VIII product page, via WebSearch snippet].
sources: Square Enix FFTCG Opus VIII product page (fftcg.square-enix-games.com/na/release/opus-viii)

trivia_bullets.text: Waltrill originates from Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles: Echoes of Time (Nintendo DS/Wii, 2009). He is a Yuke — one of the four playable races of the Crystal Chronicles subseries, characterized by armored, often helmet-masked bodies and an affinity for magic. In Echoes of Time, he is a mage-scholar who found the young Yuke Norschtalen lost in the forest and raised her as his apprentice in the village [Final Fantasy Wiki: FFCC Echoes of Time characters, via WebSearch snippet].
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki: Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles: Echoes of Time characters (finalfantasy.fandom.com)

trivia_bullets.text: Waltrill is a Backup-type card in the FFTCG system — Backup cards represent support characters who provide resources rather than entering combat directly as Forwards, fitting his lore role as a noncombatant scholar and mentor [card frontmatter: card type = backup-card].

The chibi aesthetic visible in the card art reflects the distinctive art style of the Crystal Chronicles subseries, which consistently used a rounder, more cartoonish visual language than mainline Final Fantasy titles — a deliberate departure intended to differentiate the multiplayer-focused GameCube/DS/Wii spin-off line from the cinematic prestige of FFVII through FFXII [Final Fantasy Wiki: Yuke race entry, via WebSearch snippet].

related_cards.name: Norschtalen
related_cards.note: Waltrill's ward and apprentice in Echoes of Time; the two characters are narratively inseparable

related_cards.name: Mio
related_cards.note: Another FFCC Echoes of Time character who appears in FFTCG sets

related_cards.name: Yuke (generic)
related_cards.note: Race-type cards for the Yuke tribe appear across multiple Opus sets
